description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The body is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each separated by dark, metal hoops that provide structural reinforcement. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the staves, with the top appearing to be securely closed. The wood has a natural, weathered finish, showing a mix of medium and dark brown tones, while the metal hoops are a matte, dark gray. There are no visible handles, spouts, or markings on the exterior, giving it a utilitarian and traditional appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as warehouses, alleys, or storage areas.
description: A traditional wooden barrel with dark metal hoops, bulging sides, and a closed flat top, featuring weathered brown wood and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bulging wooden staves and two dark metal hoops, with a flat, closed top.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the curvature of the staves and the continuous metal hoops, with consistent wood texture.
description_view_2: Another angled view highlighting the seamless construction of the wood and metal bands, with no visible openings or handles.
description_view_3: Side view similar to the front, emphasizing the symmetry and uniformity of the barrel's design.
